Tense funeral for Palestinian journalist Shireen Abu Akleh  TV images showed Al Jazeera reporter's coffin nearly falling to the ground as Israeli police stormed the crowd gathered in Jerusalem for her funeral.
United Arab Emirates: Mohammed bin Zayed becomes president  The ruler of Abu Dhabi was appointed president of the UAE following the death of his half-brother.
Abortion: Thousands expected at Saturday US protests  Activists for abortion rights have called for a day of demonstration.

Long Read   Roger Dachez Subscribers onlyHow the history of cathedral builders turned to legend Saturday, May 14, is the Night of the Cathedrals, across Europe. Cathedrals in medieval Europe were built by workers organized into brotherhoods, where it was said that masonry has existed since the dawn of the world.   6 min read

Long Read   Anne-Françoise Hivert Subscribers onlyUnder the kitsch of Eurovision is an undercurrent of geopolitics Officially, politics is forbidden at the world's biggest song contest. However, it regularly makes an appearance behind the scenes of the competition and even on stage.   16 min read

In Depth   Anna Politkovskaïa Anna Politkovskaya: 'Putin doesn't like people. He believes we are a means for him' The  journalist from Novaya Gazeta, Russia's last independent newspaper, published her book 'Putin's Russia' in 2004, two years before she was assassinated. 'Le Monde' publishes an extract.   6 min read

News   Assma Maad French presidential race results don't necessarily forecast upcoming legislative elections Hoping to win a majority in the Assemblée nationale, La France Insoumise emphasizes Jean-Luc Mélenchon's high turnout in the presidential election. But this projection should be taken with a grain of salt.   5 min read
